entries.problem: A Safari/iOS-only unhandled TypeError ('Load failed') occurs on every sign-out in the auth library's sign-out flow. The default SignOut path routes the external Auth0 logout through Next.js server-action redirect() chain without catching the rejection, causing Safari to surface the failed cross-origin leg as an unhandled rejection (~2.2k Sentry events). Logout still completes but generates significant noise.
entries.solution: A fix is proposed in a pull request. The PR description includes a root cause analysis, a Safari repro case, and two proposed fixes to handle the rejection from the cross-origin redirect leg in the sign-out flow.

rawMessages.text:
Hi team — we're seeing a Safari-only error in fool/fool-auth-js's sign-out flow: an `unhandled TypeError: Load` failed on every sign-out (Sentry FOOLCOM-9, ~2.2k events, Safari/iOS only). Logout still completes — it's noise, but it's noisy.

Short version: the default <SignOut> path routes the external Auth0 logout through Next's server-action redirect() chain and doesn't catch the rejection, so Safari surfaces the failed cross-origin leg as an unhandled rejection.

Full write-up — root cause, a Safari repro, and two proposed fixes — is in the PR description here: <https://github.com/themotleyfool/foolcom/pull/1149>

Would appreciate a look when someone has a moment.
Just in case, pr description here:

entries.problem: A member successfully logged in after clearing cache and cookies but received an 'Oops! Something's happened!' error when trying to access articles or My Services. The support agent wanted to rule out backend/Auth0 issues as the cause.
entries.solution: The identity services team confirmed the member's account was in good standing on the Auth0 side — login was successful and multiple services were assigned to the user. The issue was determined to be unrelated to Auth0 or account access, and was escalated to a product tech channel for premium content errors.

entries.problem: A new SA member's account was not fully loaded in the Legacy admin system, preventing a password reset link from being sent to them.
entries.solution: An admin resolved the account loading issue on the backend, after which the password reset could be performed either by the member directly or by sending them a reset link via Legacy admin.

entries.problem: A member with an active subscription was unable to log in because they had previously submitted a GDPR/DSAR deletion request, which partially deleted their account. Their account was in a half-deleted state: removed from the primary user database but still present in Backoffice (via Stripe) and the email address API, and their Auth0 account had not yet been deleted.
entries.solution: The team confirmed the member did not want their account deleted and chose to rebuild it. The resolution involved: (1) deleting the member's Auth0 account (which had been missed in the partial DSAR process and was causing a 409 conflict on re-registration), then (2) re-creating the user via the registration API (which pulled the same Account ID and UID from the still-existing email address record). The member then needed to reset their password via the login page password reset link.

entries.problem: A member purchased a subscription and received an order confirmation, but the account showed as 'Not Registered' in Admin. The password reset option was unavailable, generating an 'unable to find user' error. Investigation revealed the user did not exist in Auth0, meaning the registration process did not complete during purchase.
entries.solution: An engineer manually registered the user in Auth0/the identity system. The member was then able to reset their password via the reset password link on the login page, or a reset link could be generated through the Admin UI.

entries.problem: Admin UI password reset link generator is intermittently failing with a connection error, causing password reset links to either not generate or immediately appear as expired to members. The issue affects multiple users and is inconsistent — sometimes working, sometimes timing out.
entries.solution: Workaround is to retry the password reset link generation multiple times until it succeeds. As an alternative, manually searching the member in Legacy Admin and navigating to Auth0 from there may work. Members can also use the public-facing password reset flow on the login page. A duplicate account with the same email address may also contribute to the issue and should be resolved by liberating the email from the duplicate account. The root cause (intermittent connection/timeout errors with Auth0) was still under investigation.

entries.problem: Two members on Windows 11 laptops received an 'OOPS! There's a misconfiguration in your system or a service outage' error during login at auth.fool.com, raising concern about a possible broader outage.
entries.solution: Investigation showed no actual outage. Both users appeared to have inadvertently initiated a device passkey enrollment flow and did not complete it, causing the error. One user resolved the issue by closing and reopening the browser; the other successfully completed a password reset and logged in shortly after. The incidents were coincidental user errors, not a systemic issue.

entries.problem: A user could not access a staging environment URL because their normal login credentials did not work on the staging site.
entries.solution: The staging environment uses a separate account from production. The user needed to perform a password reset on the staging login page. If login state issues persist, signing out of both Okta and the staging site before attempting login again is recommended.

• <https://fool.okta.com/login/signout>
• <https://staging.www.fool.com/api/auth/auth0-logout>
• then try navigating to the page again <https://staging.www.fool.com/premium/databases/team-llama/ai>

if that doesn't work, can you navigate to <https://staging.www.fool.com/premium/whoami> and let me know what values you have in these fields:
```isEmployee: true|false

entries.problem: A contractor needed initial access to community-staging.fool.com but the required CommunityStaging role had not yet been migrated to the new Lumos request system.
entries.solution: Once the CommunityStaging role was added to the Lumos App Store, the steps for any staff user or contractor to gain access are: (1) Request the `CommunityStaging` role in Lumos App Store under TMF Developer and Identity. (2) Log out of both Okta and fool.com via the staging logout and Okta signout URLs. (3) Log back in to Okta through staging.www.fool.com to sync roles. (4) Navigate to community-staging.fool.com. These steps apply equally to full-time employees and contractors.

rawMessages.text: <@U02C82MRNHX> can you help <@U083ESRGQ4D> gain access to <http://community-staging.fool.com|community-staging.fool.com>?  He's never been in so needs the initial steps. Thanks.

FYI the community staging role did not make it over to the new Lumos request system from Identity Now so waiting on that to get updated for the next step, which will be:

*For ~employee~ staff user to get access to Community staging:* 
• request the role `CommunityStaging` in <https://app.lumosidentity.com/app_store?domainAppId=1685420|Lumos App Store> under TMF Developer and Identity
• log out of Okta and <http://fool.com|fool.com>
<https://staging.www.fool.com/auth/logout>
<https://fool.okta.com/login/signout>
• log _back_ in to Okta through <http://fool.com|fool.com> to get roles synced in
<https://staging.www.fool.com/auth/whoami>  → click `Employee Sign In`
• now navigate back to community staging
<https://community-staging.fool.com>

entries.problem: The staging registration API was returning 500 errors when testing new features in staging commerce gateway. The get users calls were failing due to the service not connecting to the party database.
entries.solution: The issue resolved itself after what appeared to be a database restore operation completed. The staging party database connection was restored and the service returned to normal operation around 8:20 AM EDT.
